sourceDesc.p: Derived from CDSL MW, PWG, and PWK source records for interoperability analysis.

projectDesc.p: tei-archival-profile-v0.1; TEI is used as the archival/textual model. Raw CDSL records are preserved as escaped source-entry quotes, while selected lexical structures are promoted into TEI entry, form, sense, citation, and relation elements.

revisionDesc.change: Included in the full machine-reviewed TEI profile.

sense.note: No stable machine definition could be extracted; source record is preserved in cit/quote.

listBibl.head: Machine-extracted citation index; source quotes preserve the archival text.

cit.quote: <L>47961<pc>4-0929<k1>prajApati<k2>prajA/pati {#prajA/pati#}¦ ({#pra˚ + pati#}) <lex>m.</lex> <div n="1"> 1) {%Herr der Geschöpfe%}: {#(savitA) di\vo Da\rtA Buva^nasya pra\jApa^tiH#} <ls>ṚV. 4,53,2.</ls> <is>Soma</is>, dem bildnerischen <is>Tvaṣṭar</is> verglichen <ls n="ṚV.">9,5,9.</ls> <is>Indra</is> und <is>Agni</is>: {#pra\jAnA^M pra\jApa^tI#} (so herzustellen) <ls>AV. 10,1,21.</ls> {#prajApati = divAkara#} {%Sonne%} und = {#vahni#} {%Feuer%} <ls>H. an. 4,116.</ls> <div n="1">— 2) {%ein der Zeugung vorstehender besonderer Genius%}, welcher aber in weiteren Verhältnissen auch {%ein Beschützer des Lebendigen%} ist und neben <is>Indra</is>, <is>Savitar</is> und andern Göttern angerufen wird. {#A na^H pra\jAM ja^nayatu pra\jApa^tiH#} <ls>ṚV. 10,85,43.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 10,">169,4.</ls> <ls n="ṚV. 10,">184,1.</ls> <ls>AV. 2,34,4.</ls> <ls n="AV.">3,15,6.</ls> <ls n="AV. 3,">24,7.</ls> <ls n="AV.">4,4,2.</ls> <ls n="AV. 4,">15,11.</ls> <ls n="AV.">6,11,2.</ls> <ls n="AV. 6,11,">3.</ls> <ls n="AV. 6,">68,2.</ls> <ls n="AV. 6,">69,3.</ls> <ls n="AV.">7,19,1.</ls> <ls n="AV. 7,">24,1.</ls> <ls n="AV.">14,2,13.</ls> <ls>VS. 8,10.</ls> <ls n="VS. 8,">17.</ls> <ls>M. 12,121.</ls> <ls>SUŚR. 1,311,7.</ls> <ls>BHĀG. P. 2,6,7.</ls> <div n="1">— 3) {%Herr der Geschöpfe, Schöpfer%}; <ab>Bez.</ab> eines obersten Gottes über den <is>benannten</is> Göttern der vedischen Periode. Während <is>Prajāpati</is> in diesem Sinne im <ls>ṚV.</ls> nur ein Mal vorkommt, wo ein Zusatzvers <ls n="ṚV.">10,121,10.</ls> dem im vorhergehenden gepriesenen unbekannten Gotte den Namen <is>Prajāpati</is> beilegt, ist derselbe häufig erwähnt in den jüngern Liedern des <ls>AV.</ls> und in der <ls>VS.</ls>, und allgemein angenommen in den <ls>BRĀHMAṆA.</ls> So hoch aber auch die Prädicate sind, welche diesem obersten Gotte der älteren Theologie, an dessen Stelle die Philosophie <is>Brahman</is> setzt, beigelegt werden, so ist derselbe gleichwohl in zohlreichen Legenden (<ab>vgl.</ab> die Kapitelanfänge der <ls>BRĀHMAṆA</ls> des <ls>YAJURVEDA</ls>, <ls>Ind. St. 3,309. fg.</ls>) durchaus polytheistisch behandelt. <ls>NIR. 10,42.</ls> <ls>AV. 3,10,13.</ls> <ls n="AV.">4,35,1.</ls> <ls n="AV.">8,1,17.</ls> <ls n="AV.">9,1,24.</ls> <ls n="AV.">10,7,7.</ls> {#yatpa^ra\mama^va\maM yacca^ maDya\maM pra\jApa^tiH sasf\je vi\SvarU^pam#} <ls n="AV. 3,10,">8.</ls> <ls n="AV. 3,10,">41.</ls> <ls n="AV. 3,">8,13.</ls> {#traya^striMSataM lo\kAnnira^mimIta pra\jApa^tiH#} <ls n="AV.">11,3,52.</ls> <ls>VS. 8,36.</ls> <ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,4,2,1. fgg.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">2,2,4,1.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">6,1,2,11.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">10,1,3,1.</ls> <ls>AIT. BR. 1,1.</ls> <ls n="AIT. BR. 1,">10.</ls> <ls n="AIT. BR. 1,">13.</ls> {#indro vE vftraM hatvAtravItprajApatimahametadasAni yattvamahaM mahAnasAni#} <ls n="AIT. BR.">3,21.</ls> <ls n="AIT. BR. 3,">36.</ls> {#prajApatirvE pita fBUnmartyAnsato 'martyAnkftvA tftIyasavana ABajat#} <ls n="AIT. BR.">6,12.</ls> {#prajApaterviBAnnAma lokastasmiMstvAdaDAmi#} <ls n="AIT. BR.">7,26.</ls> {#prajApatimeva praTamaM devatAnAmupasasAra#} <ls n="AIT. BR. 7,">16.</ls> <ls>TBR. 2,2,10,1.</ls> {#de\vAnA^M pi\tA ja^ni\tA pra\jAnA^m#} <ls n="TBR. 2,">8,1,3.</ls> <ls n="TBR. 2,8,1,">4.</ls> dreiunddreissig Götter, <is>Prajāpati</is> der 34ste <ls n="TBR. 2,">7,1,4.</ls> <ls>ŚAT. BR. 5,1,2,13.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">11,6,3,5.</ls> der vierte über den drei Welten <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">4,6,1,4.</ls> Vater <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">1,5,1,16.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 1,5,">3,2.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR. 1,">7,2,22.</ls> ist erster Opferer <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">2,4,4,1.</ls> <ls n="ŚAT. BR.">6,2,3,1.</ls> <ls>TS. 1,6,9,1.</ls> <ls>PAÑCAV. BR. 12,13,4. 13,4,1. 25,6,2.</ls> <ls>RĀJA-TAR. 3,443.</ls> <is>Prajāpati</is> <is>Manu</is> <ls>VS. 11,66.</ls> <ls>M. 10,78.</ls> <ls n="M.">12,123.</ls> <ls>MBH. 1,2581.</ls> <ls>R. 1,70,20.</ls> <is>Viśvakarman</is> [Page4-0930] <ls>H. an. 4,116.</ls> <ls>VS. 12,61.</ls> <ls>ŚAT. BR. 7,4,2,5</ls>; <ab>vgl.</ab> {#SilpaprajApati#} <ls>MBH. 1,2592.</ls> <is>Parameṣṭhin</is> (<ab>s. u.</ab> dem Worte, wo auch das Verhältniss von <is>Prajāpati</is> und <is>Parameṣṭhin</is> berührt wird) <ls>VS. 14,31.</ls> {#viBudAvan#} <ls>TS. 3,5,8,1.</ls> {#pitrA hi SaptAH svayA devatayA svena prajApatinA#} {%Schöpfer%} <ls>ŚĀṄKH. BR. 30,5. 11,7.</ls> {#viSve devAH prajApatirAjAnaH#} <ls>ŚĀṄKH. ŚR. 4,21,12.</ls> {#prajApatirbrahmA, vedA devAH#} <ls>ĀŚV. GṚHY. 3,4.</ls> <ls>PĀR. GṚHY. 1,12. 2,9. 3. 4.</ls> {#asyAH sargaviDO prajApatiraBUccandro nu kAntipradaH SfNgArEkarasaH svayaM nu madano mAso nu puzpAkaraH#} {%Schöpfer%} <ls>VIKR. 9.</ls> = {#brahman, viDi, viDAtar#} <ls>AK. 1,1,1,12.</ls> <ls n="AK.">3,4,18,117.</ls> <ls>TRIK. 3,3,167.</ls> <ls>H. 212.</ls> <ls>an. 4,116.</ls> <ls>MED. t. 210.</ls> <ls>HALĀY. 1,7.</ls> <ls>M. 2,76.</ls> <ls n="M. 2,">77.</ls> <ls n="M. 2,">84.</ls> <ls n="M.">4,225.</ls> <ls n="M. 4,">248.</ls> <ls n="M.">5,28.</ls> <ls n="M.">9,16.</ls> <ls n="M. 9,">46.</ls> <ls n="M. 9,">327.</ls> <ls>R. 1,44,1.</ls> <ls n="R. 1,">72,24.</ls> <ls n="R.">2,34,24.</ls> <ls>ŚĀK. 112.</ls> <ls>VARĀH. BṚH. S. 8,24.</ls> sein <is>Nakṣatra</is> ist <is>Rohiṇī</is> <ls n="VARĀH. BṚH. S.">15,28.</ls> <ls n="VARĀH. BṚH. S.">97,8.</ls> <ls>WEBER, JYOT. 94.</ls> <is>Mūla</is> <ls n="WEBER, ">Nakṣ. 2,374. 379.</ls> <is>Prajāpati</is> stellt seiner Tochter nach <ls>AIT. BR. 3,33.</ls> <ls>ŚAT. BR. 1,7,4,1.</ls> <ls>PAÑCAV. BR. 8,2,10.</ls> <ls>PRAB. 8,3.</ls> hat 33 Töchter, die er dem <is>Soma</is> giebt, <ls>TS. 2,3,5,1.</ls> <ls>KĀṬH. 11,3.</ls> {#saBA#} und {#samiti#} heissen seine Töchter <ls>AV. 7,12,1.</ls> <ls>PĀR. GṚHY. 3,13.</ls> eine seiner Töchter ist Mutter <is>Indra's</is> und <is>Soma's</is> <ls>AV. 3,10,13.</ls> <is>Prajāpati</is> zwischen <is>Brahman</is> und <is>Bṛhaspati</is> <ls>TAITT. UP. 2,8.</ls> zwischen <is>Brahman</is> und <is>Uśanas</is> <ls>VP. 272.</ls> zwischen <is>Brahman</is> und <is>Pṛthivi</is> <ls>M. 2,225.</ls> neben <is>Brahman</is> <ls>LALIT. ed. Calc. 434,10.</ls> {#dakza#} (<ab>s.</ab> auch <ab>u. d.</ab> Worte). {#marIci#} <ab>u. s. w.</ab> <ls>H. an.</ls> <ls>MED.</ls> <ls>M. 9,128.</ls> <ls>MBH. 1,4807. fgg.</ls> <ls n="MBH.">2,435. fgg.</ls> ({#prajAnAM patayaH#}). <ls n="MBH.">10,774.</ls> <ls n="MBH.">12,7571. fgg.</ls> <ls>HARIV. 40. fg.</ls> <ls n="HARIV.">2385.</ls> <ls n="HARIV.">11515.</ls> <ls>VP. 49, N. 2. 54.</ls> <ls>BHĀG. P. 3,20,9.</ls> <ls>ŚĀK. 168</ls> (<is>Kaśyapa</is>). <is>Vākya</is> <ls>Ind. St. 3,224.</ls> <ab>Bein.</ab> <ls>ŚIVA'S</ls> <ls>ŚIV.</ls> Gesetzgeber <ls>Ind. St. 1,233. 467.</ls> {#prajApaterakzaram#} oder {#AkzAram, anuSlokaH, aBIvartaH, arkaH, azwaniDanam, Ajyadoham, AtIzadIyam, kanInikam, gUrdaH#} oder {#kUrdaH, cakzuH#}.{# catustriMSatsaMmitam, trayastriMSatsaMmitam, dIrGam, dIrGAyuzyam, dohadohIyam, Danam, DarmaH, niDanakAmam, pratizWA, maDuScunniDanam, marAyam, mahoviSIyam, vazawkAraniDanam, vAjajit, viDarma, vErAjam, vratapakzO, SradDA, SrudDIyam (?), SlokaH#}.{# sadoviSIyam, saMtAnikam, sadohavirDAnam, sIdantIyam#}.{# sutaM rayizWIyam, hikavikanikam (vikanikahikam, vikavikahikam)#} und {#hfdayam#} Namen von <is>Sāman</is> <ls>Ind. St. 3,224. fg.</ls> Mehreres über <is>Prajāpati</is> kann man noch in den <ls>Ind. St.</ls> und bei <ls>MUIR, ST.</ls> finden. <div n="1">— 4) {%Herr des Volkes, König, Fürst%} <ls>TRIK.</ls> <ls>H. an.</ls> <ls>MED.</ls> <ls>HALĀY. 2,266.</ls> <div n="1">— 5) {%Schwiegersohn%} <ls>H. an.</ls> <div n="1">— 6) {%Vater%} <ls>ŚKDR.</ls> mit folgendem Beleg: {#janako janmadAnAcca rakzaRAcca pitA nfRAm . tato vistIrRakaraRAtkalayA sa prajApatiH .. iti brahmavEvarte gaRapatiKaRqe 44 aDyAyaH ..#} <div n="1">— 7) {%ein <ab>best.</ab> Insect%} ({#kIwa#}) <ls>ŚKDR.</ls> <div n="1">— 8) <ab>Bez.</ab> {%des 5ten (39ten) Jahres im 60jährigen Jupitercyclus%} <ls>VARĀH. BṚH. S. 8,29.</ls> <ls>Journ. of the Am. Or. S. VI,180.</ls> <div n="1">— 9) {%ein <ab>best.</ab> Stern%}, δ {%Aurigae%} <ls>SŪRYAS. 8,20.</ls> <div n="1">— 10) = {#kAlapuruza#} 1. <ls>YAVANEŚVARA</ls> in <ls>Z. f. d. K. d. M. 4,344, Śl. 3.</ls> <div n="1">— 11) <ab>N. pr.</ab> eines Mannes <ls>Verz. d. B. H. No. 226. 260. 1100.</ls> eines <is>Ṛṣi</is> <ls>VYUTP. 90.</ls> <div n="v">— <ab>Vgl.</ab> {#prAjApata#}.{# prAjApatya#} . <LEND>
<L>219564<pc>7-360-a<k1>prajApati<k2>prajApati {#prajApati#}¦ IV. 2〉 lies {%Frau, Matrone, Dame%} und <ab>vgl.</ab> <ls>DIVYĀVAD. 2,2</ls>. <ls n="DIVYĀVAD.">98,21</ls>.<info n="sup_7"/> <LEND>
